text.title: Trend Classification and Timing Assessment
text.summary: Trend categories, adoption curve stages, and the quantitative-anchor requirement for timing claims.

list.item: Macro (5-10 years): AI adoption, remote work normalization, demographic aging — use as context, not opportunity
Industry (2-5 years): no-code tools, creator economy, vertical SaaS — the actionable sweet spot
Micro (6-24 months): specific tool adoption, API standardization, pricing-model shift — tactical opportunity
Growth drivers: what accelerates this trend? (technology capability threshold, regulatory mandate, demographic shift)
Adoption curve position: where in the Rogers curve? — must cite a quantitative anchor, not just a label
Threats: what could reverse this trend? (competing technology, regulatory reversal, macro downturn)
Timing verdict: too early (market unaware), just right (market aware + solutions emerging), too late (established winners with moat)

rule.statement: Require a quantitative anchor for any adoption-stage claim: category revenue at the top 3 players, named buyer logos at recognizable enterprises, or published category spend data. Reject "Early Majority" labels without a quantitative anchor.
rule.rationale: Adoption-stage labels are subjective and unfalsifiable without quantitative evidence. The same trend looks "early" to a bull and "late" to a bear; quantitative anchors resolve the dispute.

text.reference: Trend analysis template with drivers, threats, and timing assessment
